body.title: PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT + PROC_INVOKE_COMMIT habits reference unregistered pe_chain:run_engram_cursor
body.result: Investigation: habits not dead. SKILL_SPRINT_ENTRY and SKILL_COMMIT_ENTRY engrams exist (created by skill_importer.py). PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT/COMMIT habits have correct triggers and intent. Missing piece is pe_chain:run_engram_cursor interpreter itself. Reframed as T-engram-cursor-runtime (L). Biomimicry verdict: useful, just needs to be hooked up — this IS the direction of moving skill execution into the matrix.
body.description:
Discovered in 2026-04-13 day-close audit (habit health step).

Two PROCEDURAL memories have code_ref='pe_chain:run_engram_cursor':
  - PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT
  - PROC_INVOKE_COMMIT

But `run_engram_cursor` is not in the tool registry (checked against registry._tools, 246 registered tools). When these habits fire, the code_ref dispatch resolves the string, looks it up in the registry, and finds nothing. Silent dead habits.

Check git log for when run_engram_cursor was removed or renamed. If renamed, update both habits' metadata.code_ref. If removed entirely, either delete both habits or pick a replacement target (the PROC_CODE_A_TICKET habit shipped with a real code_ref as a template — see that ticket's result line for the pattern).

Not blocking anything today — habits just no-op when fired. Ticket so tomorrow's cleanup has a breadcrumb.

INERTIA: LOW — metadata update in DB, no source code changes required if the right replacement exists.
